title: CustomGraphicsCoordinates Object
description: Represents coordinates that are used to define vertices in custom graphics.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

methods.name: create
methods.description: Static method that creates a CustomGraphicsCoordinates object which can be used as input to various custom graphics methods.

methods.name: getColor
methods.description: Gets the color assigned to the coordinate at the specified index.

methods.name: getCoordinate
methods.description: Gets the coordinate at the specified index.

methods.name: setColor
methods.description: Sets the color of the coordinate at the specified index.

methods.name: setCoordinate
methods.description: Sets the coordinate at the specified index.

properties.name: colors
properties.description: Gets and sets the colors associated with the coordinate data. This is used when a mesh is displayed using per-vertex coloring. The color at each vertex is represented by four values where they are the red, green, blue, and alpha values. This should contain the same number of colors as vertices.

properties.name: coordinateCount
properties.description: Returns the number of coordinates defined in the CustomGraphicsCoordinates object.

properties.name: coordinates
properties.description: Gets and sets the coordinate data associated with this CustomGraphicsCoordinates object. This data represents the x, y, z components of the coordinates where the unit of measure is centimeters.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

classType.title: CustomGraphicsCoordinates.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().
classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

create.title: CustomGraphicsCoordinates.create Method
create.description: Static method that creates a CustomGraphicsCoordinates object which can be used as input to various custom graphics methods.
create.return_description: Returns the created CustomGraphicsCoordinates object or null in the case of failure.

parameters.name: coordinates
parameters.description: An array of doubles where the values are the x, y, z components of each coordinate where the unit of measure is centimeters.

getColor.title: CustomGraphicsCoordinates.getColor Method
getColor.description: Gets the color assigned to the coordinate at the specified index.
getColor.return_description: Returns the color associated with the index. Can also return null in the case where there is no color assigned.

parameters.name: index
parameters.description: The index of the color to return. The first color has an index of 0.

getCoordinate.title: CustomGraphicsCoordinates.getCoordinate Method
getCoordinate.description: Gets the coordinate at the specified index.
getCoordinate.return_description: Returns the coordinate as a Point3D object.

parameters.name: index
parameters.description: The index of the coordinate to return. The first coordinate has an index of 0.

setColor.title: CustomGraphicsCoordinates.setColor Method
setColor.description: Sets the color of the coordinate at the specified index.
setColor.return_description: Returns true if setting the color was successful.

parameters.name: index
parameters.description: The index of the coordinate to set. The first coordinate has an index of 0.

parameters.name: color
parameters.description: The color value as a Color object.

setCoordinate.title: CustomGraphicsCoordinates.setCoordinate Method
setCoordinate.description: Sets the coordinate at the specified index.
setCoordinate.return_description: Returns true if setting the coordinate was successful.

parameters.name: index
parameters.description: The index of the coordinate to set. The first coordinate has an index of 0.

parameters.name: coordinate
parameters.description: The coordinate value as a Point3D object.

colors.title: CustomGraphicsCoordinates.colors Property
colors.description: Gets and sets the colors associated with the coordinate data. This is used when a mesh is displayed using per-vertex coloring. The color at each vertex is represented by four values where they are the red, green, blue, and alpha values. This should contain the same number of colors as vertices.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = customGraphicsCoordinates_var.colors

# Set the value of the property.
customGraphicsCoordinates_var.colors = propertyValue
